title: Future return accidentally used as model feature
Full-sample normalization before train/test split
code:
df['z_volume'] = (df['volume_usd'] - df['volume_usd'].mean()) / df['volume_usd'].std()
train = df[df['date'] < '2026-04-11']
test = df[df['date'] >= '2026-04-11']

title: Random split used for financial time series
code:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
train, test = train_test_split(df, test_size=0.25, shuffle=True, random_state=7)

title: Backtest reports gross returns without costs
code:
df['position'] = (df['close_usd'].pct_change() > 0).astype(int)
df['strategy_return'] = df['position'].shift(1) * df['close_usd'].pct_change()
sharpe = df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

title: Simple shifted momentum with explicit costs
code:
df['ret_1d'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change()
df['signal'] = (df['ret_1d'].rolling(3).mean().shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
df['turnover'] = df['signal'].diff().abs().fillna(0)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'].shift(1) * df['ret_1d'] - 0.001 * df['turnover']

title: Report claims state-of-the-art performance without baseline
code: The agentic strategy is clearly state-of-the-art and beats all alternatives. We omit baseline comparisons because the equity curve is visually compelling.
